text: The patient is positioned in modified lithotomy. A circumferential incision is made around the end ileostomy site, which is then completely mobilized and freed from surrounding structures before being returned to the abdomen with temporary closure.

The surgeon introduces a single port gel point device into the abdomen through the previous end ileostomy site. The rectum is identified, and a total mesorectal excision is performed by staying posterior. Lateral attachments are taken down, followed by anterior dissection. The uterus is retracted anteriorly to access the pelvis and protect it from damage, with mild entry into the vaginal wall observed on the left side.

The surgeon laparoscopically repairs the vaginal wall entry using 3-0 Vicryl sutures in a figure-of-eight pattern. Dissection proceeds down to the pelvic floor, where the rectum is stapled off using a GIA-A-Sixty stapler, confirmed by bimanual examination. The liver, gallbladder, right kidney, and duodenum are visible in the surgical field, highlighting the challenge of switching orientation from the pelvis to the upper abdomen during single incision laparoscopic surgery.

The surgeon carefully separates the retroperitoneal attachments of the superior mesenteric artery (SMA) to mobilize it, ensuring the ileum can reach the inferior border of the pubic symphysis for a tension-free pouch anastomosis. The SMA is mobilized down to the pancreas, freeing the ileum for the procedure.

The surgeon creates a J-pouch by performing a side-to-side anastomosis using two fires of IL-A-hundred. The tip of the J-pouch is stapled off with TA-thirty and over-sewn. A leak test is conducted before introducing the J-pouch into the abdomen, followed by placing a purse string suture with two-zero proline. EEA-thirty-one is used for the pouch anal anastomosis. Pouchoscopy is performed to check for anastomotic leaks, which are negative. A loop of bowel, forty centimeters proximal to the pouch, is brought to the skin, and a stoma is matured in place. The resected rectal specimen is visible.
